title: salix viminalis subsp. gmelinii
intro: Salix viminalis subsp. gmelinii, also known as Gmelin's willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The medicinal properties of Gmelin's willow are said to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water to create a tea or decoction that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
